title: helmiopsis pseudopopulus
intro: Helmiopsis pseudopopulus, also known as the false poplar or false cottonwood, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antibacterial agent. Helmiopsis pseudopopulus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use, and its roots and leaves are often used in traditional African medicine.
